interaction: Risk of serotonin syndrome — rapid-onset, potentially life-threatening. Avoid combination; require 14-day washout.
Increased bleeding risk via platelet inhibition + GI mucosal damage. Avoid combination. Acetaminophen preferred for pain.
Increased bleeding. Low-dose aspirin (81 mg) for cardiac indication may be acceptable with INR monitoring per cardiology guidance.
CYP3A4 inhibition increases statin exposure → rhabdomyolysis risk. Hold simvastatin during macrolide course or use atorvastatin/rosuvastatin with caution.
CYP3A4 inhibition by grapefruit increases statin exposure. Avoid grapefruit juice or limit to 1 cup/day with statin pharmacy guidance.
Hyperkalemia risk. Monitor potassium + creatinine; avoid potassium supplements + salt substitutes containing KCl.
Bronchospasm risk. Use cardioselective beta-1 (metoprolol, atenolol, bisoprolol) if beta-blocker indicated.
Hepatotoxicity if cumulative dose > 4 g/day (chronic) or 7.5 g (acute). Many OTC cold/flu products contain acetaminophen. Read labels.
Serotonin syndrome + seizure threshold lowering. Use alternative analgesic. Seizure risk additive with bupropion.
Respiratory depression + death. FDA black-box warning. Avoid combination unless no alternative + closely monitor.
Amiodarone increases warfarin effect; INR rise within 1-2 weeks. Reduce warfarin dose 30-50% on amiodarone initiation; monitor INR weekly.
P-glycoprotein inhibition raises digoxin levels → toxicity (nausea, arrhythmia, visual changes). Halve digoxin + monitor levels.
Lactic acidosis risk if AKI develops post-contrast. Hold metformin at time of contrast; restart 48 hours post if renal function stable.
Xanthine oxidase inhibition raises 6-MP/azathioprine 75%-fold → severe myelosuppression. Reduce 6-MP/azathioprine 75% if combination required.
CYP3A4 + P-glycoprotein induction → reduced drug levels. Contraceptive failure, transplant rejection, HIV viral rebound. Counsel against St. John's Wort with these meds.
